title: The Day of Wrath
content:
Day of Satan's painful duty!
 Earth shall vanish, hot and sooty;
 So says Virtue, so says Beauty.

content:

title: The New Decalogue
content:
Have but one God: thy knees were sore
If bent in prayer to three or four.

Adore no images save those
The coinage of thy country shows.

Take not

title: Freedom by Ambrose Bierce — Inspirational Poetry Lines
content:
Freedom, as every schoolboy knows,
Once shrieked as Kosciusko fell;
On every wind, indeed, that blows
I hear her yell.

title: Weather
content:
Once I dipt into the future far as human eye could see,
And I saw the Chief Forecaster, dead as any one can be
Dead and damned and shut in Hades as

title: With A Book
content:
Words shouting, singing, smiling, frowning
Sense lacking.
Ah, nothing, more obscure than Browning,
Save blacking.

title: The Statesmen
content:
How blest the land that counts among
Her sons so many good and wise,
To execute great feats of tongue
When troubles rise.

title: Christian
content:
I dreamed I stood upon a hill, and, lo!
The godly multitudes walked to and fro
Beneath, in Sabbath garments fitly clad,

title: To The Bartholdi Statue
content:
O Liberty, God-gifted,
Young and immortal maid,
In your high hand uplifted,
The torch declares your trade.

Its crimson menace, flaming
Upon the

title: The Mad Philosopher
content:
The flabby wine-skin of his brain
Yields to some pathologic strain,
And voids from its unstored abysm
The driblet of an aphorism.

title: An Inscription
content:
A conqueror as provident as brave,
He robbed the cradle to supply the grave.
His reign laid quantities of human dust:
He fell upon the just and the

title: Piety
content:
The pig is taught by sermons and epistles
To think the God of Swine has snout and bristles.

title: Rimer
content:
The rimer quenches his unheeded fires,
The sound surceases and the sense expires.
Then the domestic dog, to east and west,
Expounds the passions bu

title: Decalogue
content:
Thou shalt no God but me adore:
'Twere too expensive to have more.

No images nor idols make
For Roger Ingersoll to break.

Take not God's name in

title: Elegy
Alone
content:
In contact, lo! the flint and steel,
By sharp and flame, the thought reveal
That he the metal, she the stone,
Had cherished secretly alone. 

Bool
